title: Timonius scabriflorus
taxonomy: Timonius scabriflorus is classified within the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the melastome family. This family consists of approximately 170 genera and 5,000 species of flowering plants. Melastomataceae is part of the order Myrtales, which includes other families such as the myrtle and staphylocarpus families. The order Myrtales is a part of the clade Rosids, which also includes families like the rose and apple families. The clade Rosids is a subdivision of the larger clade Eurosids, which consists of various flowering plants.
uses: Timonius scabriflorus is a plant species known for its various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. Its leaves and stems are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. In some parts of the world, the plant is also used to treat wounds and skin infections. The medicinal properties of Timonius scabriflorus are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of this plant species.

intro: Timonius scabriflorus is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the Rough-flowered Timonius. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising remedy for various conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Timonius scabriflorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
